p1: I photograph people and places. Through street and documentary photography, I try to capture the unscripted moments that reveal how we inhabit, shape, and are shaped by the spaces around us.
p2: My background in urban planning, culture, and data gives me a particular way of seeing — trained on spatial patterns and how communities form. But the camera asks for something different: presence, patience, and instinct. SHAAR Studio is where those two ways of seeing meet.
p3: I care deeply about people, peace, and the environments we share. Photography, for me, is an act of paying attention — of honoring the ordinary and insisting that it matters.
tagline: Based in Boston. Looking at every city.
